bull.t: Recurring Subscription Model And Upsell-driven Revenue
bull.b: A subscription, user-based model with modular add-ons creates durable recurring revenue and predictable renewal/expansion dynamics. Upsell across SSO, MFA, lifecycle and CIAM drives expansion revenue and increases customer lifetime value, supporting steady cash flows and scalable margins.

bull.t: High Gross Margins And Improving Profitability
bull.b: Sustained ~77% gross margins plus a return to operating profit indicate structural unit economics strength in cloud IAM. Improving margins suggest operating leverage from scale and product mix, enabling durable profitability even if top-line growth moderates.

bull.t: Strong Balance Sheet And Healthy Cash Generation
bull.b: Large cash reserves, consistent operating cash flow and positive free cash flow provide financial flexibility for R&D, strategic partnerships, buybacks, or M&A. Low leverage reduces solvency risk and supports multi-quarter investment in product and go-to-market priorities.

bear.t: Moderating Revenue Growth And Modest Net Margin
bear.b: While revenue has expanded, growth is more moderate than earlier high-growth years and net margin remains modest for a software company. This limits free-cash-flow upside and investor cushion if sales cycles lengthen or enterprise spending moderates.

AI agents present strategic upside but remain early-stage with unclear pricing models and measurement methods. Rising inference/tooling costs can compress margins as usage scales, creating execution and ROI risk until mature billing and cost-pass-through mechanisms exist.
